name: Nifty 50
summary: Day 60 Monday — BRANCH 3 RESOLUTION → INDIA BEST-POSITIONED OUTCOME REALIZED. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia, defeating Branch 1 and 2. Friday close still operative pre-Indian cash open: Nifty 23,897.95 (-1.1% 1D, +3.4% 1M, -5.1% 3M); Sensex 76,664.21 (-1.3%); Bank Nifty 56,089.75 (-0.4%); USD/INR ₹94.22; India 10Y 6.98%. Branch 3 Monday setup: Nifty likely opens 24,000-24,200 area on +0.5-1.5% rebound (60% probability) — Nifty 23,775 floor likely holds; OMCs IOC/BPCL/HPCL likely +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery as crude input cost compresses; USD/INR likely tests ₹93.80-94.10 strengthening band on capital-flow improvement; India 10Y likely 6.80-6.85% on inflation-expectation easing. RBI special-credit-facility for OMC dollar buying (Reuters April 26 RBI ask) confirms India preparing for sustained $100+ Brent regime — but Branch 3 reduces immediate severity. Asian Monday LIVE chip-led: Nikkei +1.4%, KOSPI +2.2%, TAIEX +2.7% — Indian IT services likely modest beneficiary. FII deceleration crystallises into entry: Elara Capital +$106M Friday positive flow + weekly outflow $1.2B → $180M decelerating; Branch 3 confirmation likely converts deceleration into net inflow into May. SMH +5.1% Friday is independent of crisis (Samsung April 30 binary still operative). India structurally worst-positioned for Branch 1 architecture has been retired; best-positioned for Branch 3 architecture is now operative. For 3-5 year horizon investor: deployment window remains open; entry into 24,000-24,200 area on Branch 3 rebound is rational on conviction-after-mapping framework — though contrarian-extreme entry at 23,500 is now low-probability.

price_snapshots.delta_context: Already down 5.2% YTD before full war impact

price_snapshots.trigger: Asia selloff on Trump power plant threats + FPI selling
price_snapshots.delta_context: Lowest since April 2025. ₹14T market cap wiped.

price_snapshots.trigger: Technical bounce from oversold levels, DII buying
price_snapshots.delta_context: Recovered +399 points (+1.8%). Still down 10.4% from one month ago.

price_snapshots.trigger: FY26's last trading day — worst close
price_snapshots.delta_context: Deepened from morning -0.9% to -2.14% as oil surged back to $115+ and rupee NOP rally reversed. MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%.
+4.1% 1D, +940 points from ~22,970 base; Bank Nifty leading at +5.0%; 200-DMA within striking distance at 24,051

price_snapshots.trigger: Ceasefire rally confirmed close — briefly touched 200-DMA at ~24,051
price_snapshots.delta_context: +3.85% from 23,124 base; all 50 stocks green; Bank Nifty +5.0%; FII selling into rally (Rs 8,692 cr) offset by DII + short-covering

price_snapshots.trigger: Ceasefire fracture sellback — Day 2 gives back gains
price_snapshots.delta_context: From 24,013 Apr 8 close to 23,775 Apr 9 — failed to hold 200-DMA at ~24,051. Bank Nifty -1.6%. Sensex -1.2%.
Intraday high 24,074 briefly above. Bank Nifty +2.0% leading. INR divergence (Rs93.05 weaker despite rally) undermines sustainability.

price_snapshots.trigger: Crossed above 200-DMA (24,050) — first time since April 10
price_snapshots.delta_context: First ALIGNED Nifty-INR session since ceasefire day. Relief rally on deal hopes and oil below $100.

price_snapshots.trigger: Highest post-ceasefire close; Sensex 78,493 recovered all but 1.7% of crisis losses
price_snapshots.delta_context: +0.65% from 24,196.75 Apr 16 close. INR strengthened to Rs92.84 on Brent crash. Bank Nifty +0.9%. Most fragile position in market: long Indian equities going into 72-hour weekend across Sunday Apr 19 Iran waiver expiry.

price_snapshots.trigger: STRUCTURAL RESILIENCE — Nifty flat on a day everything pointed down 1-2% (Brent +5%, Europe -1.1%, US -0.24%, USD/INR weakening)
price_snapshots.delta_context: Cumulative FII outflow Rs 1.61 lakh crore (~$17.3B at ₹93.05) since Feb 28 fully offset by domestic MF buying; BSE market cap at pre-war levels despite 8-week kinetic phase. ICICI Q4 absorbed crisis-premium without margin compression. 200-DMA 24,050 trigger STRUCTURALLY INSULATED: activation requires compounding event (Wed ceasefire lapse + earnings miss + FII acceleration), not single shock. Domestic-demand absorption = 'visible hand' layer with 3-6 month persistence post-resolution. Raises deployment-opportunity hurdle: Nimit's conviction-entry condition now narrows materially.

price_snapshots.trigger: DAY 5 DECLINE; broke 24,000 psychological floor; FPI YTD ₹1.67T
price_snapshots.delta_context: April 9 low 23,775 only 0.5% below Friday close. Branch 3 setup (Brent gap-down to $100.47) likely arrests the decline at 23,775 floor — Monday Apr 27 likely opens 24,000-24,200 area on rebound (60% prob); USD/INR tests ₹93.80-94.10 strengthening band; OMCs IOC/BPCL/HPCL likely +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery. India 10Y likely 6.80-6.85%.

signals.content: Nifty futures dropped 1.7% on first trading day after strikes. Markets declined ~4% within two days. Goldman warned India 'most impacted in Asia.'
FIIs net sold ~₹52,704 crore in March. Single-day outflow ₹10,717 crore (largest of 2026). Societe Generale expects India underperformance to deepen. DII/SIP flows partially offsetting.

Nifty bounced +1.8% to 22,912 — technical recovery from April 2025 lows reached yesterday. DII buying provided support. But the bounce masks underlying weakness: FPIs sold ₹82,700 crore in March (largest monthly outflow since Oct 2024), India's PMI slowed to lowest since Oct 2022, and IPO market remains frozen. Total Indian investor losses since war began: ₹48 lakh crore (~$511 billion). The 'second only to Covid' framing in Indian media (Business Today) signals depth of concern.

Nifty fell 2.1% to 22,820 on Houthi war entry day. Sensex -2.2% to 73,583. FPIs pulled Rs 88,180 crore ($11.5B) in March. Critical support at 22,500 (200-day EMA). Bernstein warns Nifty below 20,000 if conflict persists through 2026.

Monday open: -0.9% to 22,612 despite rupee rally. Market seeing through mechanical NOP unwinding — oil at $107 still elevated, FPI selling continues. Nifty decoupled from rupee in this regime: both need OIL to fall, not just regulatory currency fix.

EVENING UPDATE: Nifty crashed from morning's 22,612 (-0.9%) to close at 22,331.40 (-2.14%, -488 points). Sensex settled at 71,947.55 (-2.22%, -1,635 points). This was FY26's LAST TRADING DAY — fiscal year ended on its worst note. Broader markets worse: MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%. Worst sectors: PSU Banks, Bank Nifty, Financial Services. Top losers: Bajaj Finance, Shriram Finance, SBI. FPI outflows hit record ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March. The oil reversal ($107→$115) and rupee collapse (93.56→95.22) during Indian trading hours drove the deepening selloff. India entered FY26 as the world's fastest-growing major economy; it ended the fiscal year with record FPI outflows, record-low rupee, and Nifty -13.9% from peak. (Sources: Business Standard, Liquide, 5paisa, NSDL)

Nifty closed at 22,331 (-2.1%) — lowest since April 2025. FY26 ended as worst fiscal year since COVID. FPI outflows ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March (RECORD). MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%. Bank Nifty -3.8% (worst sector) on NOP cap blowback. FY26 ended -13.9% from peak. Split: ~70% crisis-driven (reversible), ~30% structural (earnings slowdown). Key level: 22,000 (200-day EMA). GIFT Nifty recovered from -1.84% to +0.16% for April 1 opening. Bernstein four scenarios: 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). CONFIRMED.

FY27 Day 1 (April 1): Nifty closed at 22,679.40 (+1.6%) — India joined global relief rally but massively underperformed vs KOSPI +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%, S&P +2.9%. Brent crash -14% to $101.76 provided partial relief for import bill. Bank Nifty +2.3% recovering from March 30's -3.8% NOP carnage. Three-layer structural problem persists: (1) Crisis-reversible ~65%: oil at $101.76 still above $100, FPI record outflows ₹1.14 lakh crore in March, INR at Rs93.43. (2) Structural ~25%: earnings slowdown, record FY27 borrowing ₹17.2T, HDFC Bank NIM compression. (3) Regulatory ~10%: NOP cap forcing $30-40B unwinding (deadline April 10), STT on futures raised 0.02%→0.05%, options 0.10%→0.15%. India 10Y breached 7% (first since July 2024). India VIX at 27.89. 200-day EMA at 22,000 — 3.1% below. Bernstein scenarios: 27,500 (de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). Even with ceasefire + $85 oil, recovers only ~65% of losses. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, Bloomberg, NSDL, TradingEconomics, yfinance)

REPORTED (single source): Nifty at 23,306 (+1.72%) on April 2 per TradingEconomics — another strong rally day if confirmed. Combined with April 1's +1.6%, Sensex has gained ~2,900 points from the lows. Indian media tone shifted from "anxious" to "cautiously optimistic" on Brent crash to $101 and peace signals. (Sources: TradingEconomics)

India UNDERPERFORMED major markets — Nifty +1.6% vs KOSPI +8.44%, S&P +2.9% on April 1. Three-layer damage that de-escalation cannot fully fix: crisis-reversible (~65%), structural (~25%), regulatory (~10%). 200-day EMA at 22,000 = critical support (3.1% below). Bernstein range: 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). (Sources: multiple market data)

Nifty fell -2.0% to 22,217 on April 2 as Trump's primetime address ('hit Iran extremely hard') reversed the global peace rally. Bank Nifty worse at -2.7% to 50,080. The April 1 recovery (+1.56% close) was a false dawn — India gave back the gains and then some. Oil at $107+ keeps import bill pressure intense. FPI selling continues (₹8,331 cr net sold on April 1 alone). April 6-8 RBI MPC is the next domestic catalyst. (Sources: yfinance, Business Standard, BusinessToday)

Nifty closed April 2 at 22,713 — weekly -2.5% (-593 points). Sensex 73,319 (-2.6%, -1,953 pts). Markets CLOSED April 3 for Good Friday. FPIs sold ₹9,931 crore on April 2 alone — ACCELERATION from ₹8,331 crore on April 1. Total 2026 FPI outflows now ~₹1.31 lakh crore. 200-DMA at 22,000 remains critical — 3.2% buffer from April 2 close. RBI MPC April 6-8 is next catalyst. Oil at $109-112 devastating for import bill. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, NSDL)

Nifty 22,713 (+0.15% on April 3) — flat session, range 22,183-22,782. Markets CLOSED April 4 (Good Friday) and April 5 (Saturday). Bank Nifty -14.8% 1M — worst Indian sector by far. FPIs pulled Rs 19,837 crore in first two April sessions (Rs 8,331 cr Apr 1 + Rs 9,931 cr Apr 2 — ACCELERATING). 200-DMA at 22,000 remains critical — only 1% buffer (3.2% from April 2 close). Monday April 7 absorbs weekend developments in single session: F-15 loss, Kuwait/Bahrain strikes, April 6 energy pause deadline outcome. RBI MPC starts April 6 (decision April 8). Gap risk is extreme — all weekend news priced in one open. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, NSDL, yfinance)

GIFT Nifty at 22,722 (-349.50 points from Nifty cash close of 22,602) — signals modest gap-up but this was gathered BEFORE Tuesday deadline outcome. Monday April 7 open absorbs: deadline extension to Tuesday, OPEC+ 206K decision, Kuwait/Bahrain/UAE strikes, RBI MPC Day 1. FPI outflows ACCELERATING: Rs 8,331 cr (Apr 1) + Rs 9,931 cr (Apr 2) = Rs 19,837 cr in 2 sessions. THREE sequential binary events: (1) Tuesday April 7 deadline (8 PM ET = 5:30 AM IST Apr 8 — impacts opening), (2) April 8 RBI MPC decision, (3) April 10 NOP cap deadline. Worst case: hawkish MPC + deadline expiry + NOP forced selling = Bank Nifty tests 48,000-49,000. Best case: dovish MPC + extension + NOP completion = Bank Nifty 53,000-54,000. NSE/BSE may hold special Saturday session April 11. Bank Nifty -14.1% monthly — worst sector.

Nifty 50 rallied +255 (+1.12%) to 22,968 after 3-day market closure, driven by ceasefire proposal reports. Bank Nifty +1,060 (+2.1%) to 52,609 — most beaten-down sector (-12.1% monthly) bouncing hardest. This is an OVERSOLD BOUNCE, not a recovery signal. Bank Nifty has three sequential binaries this week: (1) MPC decision April 8, (2) NOP cap deadline April 10, (3) Tuesday deadline outcome. Worst case: Bank Nifty tests 48,000-49,000. Best case: 53,000-54,000. Nifty 200-DMA at ~24,051 — current level 4.5% below, significant technical discount. India VIX at 25.52. GIFT Nifty at 23,048-23,066 suggests flat-to-positive next open. DII buying providing floor support. (Sources: Yahoo Finance, Trading Economics, BusinessToday)

Nifty 50 April 6 close 22,968.25 (+255.15 / +1.12%); Sensex 74,106.85 (+787.30 / +1.07%); Bank Nifty +2% to 52,609. Intraday low 22,542.95 (pricing the GIFT Nifty -350 panic) before recovering on US-Iran ceasefire framework reports. Markets BOUGHT the Axios story BEFORE Iran rejected it. The recovery is fragile — single-event print, easily reversible. GIFT Nifty open April 7: 23,048 (+0.20%) — building on the rebound, but Tuesday faces the risk of full unwind once Iran rejection news is fully digested. Bank Nifty's +2% leadership is instructive: financials are most exposed to oil-driven inflation but also benefit most from ceasefire-driven rate cut hopes. The asymmetry: if the deal fails, financials bleed worst.

EVENING UPDATE: April 6 ceasefire-hope rally REVERSED — Nifty closed ~22,851 (-0.51%), intraday low 22,719. Bank Nifty hardest hit at ~51,549 (-2.0% from 52,609). Session in three acts: crash open, partial recovery to 23,006, then fade. Only 14/50 Nifty stocks green. India VIX 25.47 (+5.2%). FIIs sold Rs 8,167cr April 6; FII index futures net short -2.68 lakh contracts. IT outperformed (+2.06%), financials led losses. Markets bought the Axios ceasefire story BEFORE Iran rejected it — now unwinding. Three sequential binaries remain: MPC April 8, NOP cap April 10, deadline outcome tonight. (Sources: Upstox, IBTimes India, 5Paisa)

NIFTY +4.1% TO 23,911 — TRIPLE BINARY COLLAPSING [CONFIRMED]. Nifty 50 cash market at 23,911.75 (+4.1%), Sensex 77,438.10 (+4.5%), Bank Nifty 55,221.95 (+5.0% — leading the rally). All three Indian binaries de-risked simultaneously: deadline tonight (RESOLVED via ceasefire), RBI MPC tomorrow (now dovish-tone-friendly with Brent at $94), NOP cap Friday (mechanical pressure eased by INR strengthening). FII flows on April 7 were still net negative -₹8,336 crore but DII +₹7,449 crore. Today's rally is partly short-covering plus the first ceasefire-day re-entry. CRITICAL LEVEL: Nifty 24,051 = 200-day EMA. Closing above this would technically negate the entire war drawdown. Bank Nifty leading +5.0% — most leveraged sector to lower oil → lower inflation → lower yields → better NIM + FII rotation back into financials. ~75% of the move is crisis-linked (reverses on Friday talks failure); ~15% is FII short-cover; ~10% is non-crisis (TCS results week, RBI dovish).

Nifty 50 confirmed close at 24,012.95 (+3.85%) on April 8 — web-confirmed figure (yfinance showed stale 23,123.65). Intraday high 24,025 briefly touched 200-DMA at ~24,051 before closing just below. Sensex 77,562.90 (+3.95%, biggest single-day gain in 6 years). Bank Nifty +5.0% to 55,221 — most leveraged to oil relief via lower inflation → rate cut hopes → NIM expansion. ALL 50 Nifty stocks closed green. Rs 16.8 lakh crore wealth created. Rally driven by short-covering + DII buying (Rs 7,979 cr) — FIIs sold Rs 8,692 crore, the flip did NOT happen. (Source: Upstox, Moolaah, GoodReturns, NSE data)

India VIX crashed 20% to 19.70 on April 8 — pre-war level. If Friday Islamabad talks fail or ceasefire collapses further, VIX at 19.70 means NO protective hedging — the reversal would be amplified. 5paisa preview for April 9: 'markets may open on a cautious note.' (Source: Bloomberg, 5paisa)

Nifty 50 closed at 23,775.10 (-0.9%) on April 9 — giving back ceasefire rally gains as fracture news hit overnight. Sensex 76,632 (-1.2%). Bank Nifty -1.6% to 54,822 (worst sector, most leveraged to oil-rate-NIM pathway). Drivers: (1) Israel Operation Eternal Darkness (254 killed in Lebanon), (2) Iran re-closed Hormuz, (3) oil bouncing +4% toward $99 restoring India CAD pressure. FII selling continued at Rs 8,692 cr on ceasefire day — rally was short-covering + DII (Rs 7,979 cr), NOT foreign re-entry. VIX at 19.70 = complacency. 200-DMA at ~24,051 not held. (Sources: yfinance, Upstox, Trendlyne FII/DII data, 5paisa)

Nifty 50 closed at 24,050.60 (+1.2%) — RIGHT AT 200-DMA at 24,051 [CONFIRMED, NSE India]. Intraday high 24,074 (briefly ABOVE). This is the first touch of 200-DMA since early March (pre-war). Bank Nifty +2.0% to 55,913 (intraday high 55,978) — most leveraged sector leading [CONFIRMED, Multibagg]. Rally driven by Brent -1.5% to $94.45 (below $95) easing India import bill + RBI dovish guidance + NOP cap deadline passing. FII selling DECELERATED: Rs 1,711 cr on April 9 (vs Rs 8,692 peak) — but flow flip NOT happened [CONFIRMED, NSE FII/DII]. INR divergence (Rs93.05 weaker despite rally) undermines sustainability. Key level: two consecutive closes ABOVE 200-DMA = technical confirmation of war drawdown negation. Today was AT it, not above.

Nifty at 200-DMA (24,051) heading into Monday with Islamabad talks failure priced in over the weekend. Bank Nifty +2.0% on Friday is the MOST VULNERABLE position — rallied on Brent below $96 plus rate cut hopes, faces sharp reversal if Brent gaps above $97+ (rate cut pathway immediately questioned). INR divergence persists: rupee weakened to Rs93.05 (+0.8%) DESPITE Nifty rallying +1.2% — fourth consecutive session of Nifty-INR divergence, indicating FII selling continues (equity rally is DII-funded, not foreign re-entry). Talks failure = potential 200-DMA break Monday. If Brent gaps to $100+, Nifty -1.5 to -3.0% depending on scenario severity, with Bank Nifty worst hit at -2 to -4%.

AFTERNOON RISK UPGRADE: Indian markets CLOSED Tuesday April 14 (Ambedkar Jayanti) -- Monday positions locked for 48 hours. The biggest weekly gain in 5 years (~6% surge) is now the setup for maximum reversal potential. VIX mispricing (19.23) means NO protective hedging in place -- the reversal will be amplified. The DII-only nature of the rally (FIIs still selling Rs 48,213 crore in April's first 10 days) means there is no foreign bid to cushion Monday selling. INR-Nifty divergence now four consecutive sessions -- strongest signal that the rally is fragile. Probability-weighted expected move: Nifty ~-1.5% to ~23,700. If CPI comes hot (>3.5%) simultaneously with oil gap, Bank Nifty -3 to -4%. (Sources: DD News, Free Press Journal, Goodreturns, market data)

Nifty crashed -1.3% to 23,744 on US naval blockade of Iranian ports. Intraday low 23,571 (-1.99%) before partial recovery. 200-DMA BROKEN — first close below since touching it on April 10. Bank Nifty -1.7% to 54,972 — Friday's +2.0% rally fully reversed. HDFC Bank -2.22%, Kotak -2.18%, SBI -1.98%. Brent above $102 destroys rate cut thesis. Markets CLOSED Tuesday April 14 (Ambedkar Jayanti) — positions locked 48 hours. FII CY2026 outflows Rs 1.71 lakh crore. The biggest weekly gain in 5 years partially reversed in single session. (Sources: NSE India, ANI, Business Standard)

Markets CLOSED April 14 (Ambedkar Jayanti). April 13 close: Nifty 23,842.65 (-0.86%), intraday low 23,556 (-1.99%) before partial recovery. Sensex recovered from -1,613 to close -702 at 76,848. Bank Nifty 55,605 (-0.6%). VIX +9.36%. Overnight development: oil fell back below $100 on Trump's CLAIMED 'Iran reached out' statement (unverified by Iran) and AP report of possible Thursday talks via Pakistan/Turkey/Egypt. S&P 500 +1.0% to 6,886 erasing all war losses. If oil holds sub-$100 through April 15, Nifty may open to relief (+0.5-1.0% gap-up). If oil gaps back above $100: flat-to-negative. FIIs sold Rs 48,213 crore in April's first 11 days; FY26 total Rs 1.79 lakh crore. JPMorgan cut Nifty target to 27,000. March CPI 3.40% — below consensus, marginally positive for rate cut pathway. (Sources: NSE India, Business Today, ET, market data)

24,194 (+1.5%) — reopened ABOVE 200-DMA (24,050) for first time since April 10. Markets closed April 14 (Ambedkar Jayanti); April 15 reopening absorbed 48 hours of positive developments: oil below $100, S&P +1.2%, DXY at 98.16. Bank Nifty +1.3% to 56,355 (financials most leveraged to rate expectations). First ALIGNED Nifty-INR session since ceasefire day (INR -1.8% strengthening). Relief rally, NOT recovery: ~80% crisis-linked and reverses if talks fail. FII flow flip has NOT happened — Rs 48,213 crore sold in April's first 11 days, CY2026 total Rs 1.79 lakh crore. Rally remains DII-funded, which historically runs out faster. Two consecutive closes above 200-DMA would be first technical confirmation of war drawdown negation. JPMorgan cut target to 27,000 (implying 12% upside even optimistically). (Sources: yfinance, market data, JPMorgan)

EVENING UPDATE: Nifty closed 24,231 (+1.6%) — ABOVE 200-DMA (24,050) for first time since April 10. First ALIGNED Nifty-INR session since ceasefire day (April 8), breaking 4-session divergence where Nifty rose but INR fell. Oil-sensitive sectors led: OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) all green. BUT: FII flow flip has NOT happened (Rs 48,213-48,905 crore sold in April's first 11 days; CY2026 Rs 1.79 lakh crore). Rally is DII-funded — historically runs out faster. Triple squeeze NOT priced: Russian waiver EXPIRED April 11, Iran waiver expires April 19, blockade continuing. JPMorgan cut Nifty target to 27,000. Two consecutive closes above 200-DMA needed for technical confirmation — today is Day 1. Reversibility EXTREME (~80% crisis-linked). (Sources: yfinance, NSE, Bloomberg)

Nifty +0.3% to 24,300 / Sensex +0.7% to 78,682 / Bank Nifty +0.3% to 56,499. MATERIALLY LAGGING Asia: Nikkei +2.2% (Takaichi 6.4T yen plan), KOSPI +1.9%, Taiwan +0.6%. Triple squeeze specific to India binding: Iran waiver expires April 19 (3 days — Treasury confirmed no extension April 15), Russian waiver expired April 11, blockade Day 4. Reliance 4 additional Iranian vessels won't complete before April 19. USD/INR Rs93.29 WEAKENED +0.1% vs yesterday's Rs93.39 — tailwind faded. FII +Rs923 crore April 15 single-session vs CY2026 >Rs1.79 lakh crore SOLD = technical short-cover, NOT structural re-entry (Business Standard analytical framing). Rally DII-funded — cannot sustain leadership if FIIs keep selling. 24,050-24,300 range compression at 200-DMA. Binary events: Iran waiver expiry April 19; Round 2 announcement April 17-20. Reversibility HIGH — 60% crisis-linked. Waiver expiry w/o extension: Nifty -1.5 to -2.5%. Round 2 announcement: Nifty +1-2%. (Sources: BS, Livemint, CNBC India, yfinance)

NIFTY POST-CEASEFIRE HIGH AT 24,353.55 — BUT WAIVER EXPIRES IN 48 HOURS [CONFIRMED]. Nifty 50 closed April 17 at 24,353.55 vs 24,196.75 on April 16, a +156 point / +0.65% gain — highest close since the April 8 ceasefire. Sensex +0.65% to 78,493.54 (+505 points), Bank Nifty +0.9% to 56,565.70. USD/INR settled Rs92.84 vs Rs93.39 April 16 — rupee strengthened ~55 paise on Brent crash to $96.16 (-3.3% net) and ceasefire optimism. Sensex recovered all but ~1.7% of crisis-induced losses from pre-crisis ~79,800 reference. The India narrative: ceasefire-optimism + Brent relief + Nifty breaking 24,300 resistance = all tactical. Counterweight NOT priced: April 19 (Sunday) Iran waiver expiry (Bessent confirmed no extension); Russian waiver already expired Apr 11; RBI deploying administrative tools (NDF ban, NOP $100M cap, state-refiner forex restrictions); Finance ministry 'considerable downside risk' warning. Monday open will test whether cumulative ceasefire trade absorbs waiver hit across 72-hour weekend. Most fragile position in market: long Indian equities going into weekend on rhetoric-driven rally. (Sources: yfinance ^NSEI/^BSESN/INR=X CONFIRMED)

Day 52 Sunday / WAIVER T-0. TRIPLE MONDAY HEADWIND CONVERGES: (1) Iran SNSC re-closure of Hormuz + $1/bbl fees institutionally ratified Saturday; (2) India MEA summoned Iran's Ambassador Saturday evening — first formal India-Iran démarche of crisis; (3) Brent reversal expected +5-8% (markets.md probability distribution center). Primary transmission chain: SNSC re-closure + fees + MEA summons → IOC/Reliance waiver door closes Monday 05:30 IST → Iranian-crude supply = zero → OMCs substitute at spot Brent $94-97 → CAD widens → rupee pressure → FPI outflow acceleration → Nifty gap-down. Monday scenarios per markets.md C2: Base 50% 24,050-24,200 (-0.6 to -1.2%); Stress 30% 23,850-24,000 (-1.4 to -2.0%, breaks 200-DMA); Benign 20% 24,300-24,400 flat-up if Islamabad delegation confirmed or CENTCOM calm statement. GIFT Nifty last reading 24,186 (Apr 16 close) — 167pt gap with cash; Monday opens first reconciles, then absorbs SNSC/MEA re-pricing. Secondary headwinds: Q4 FY26 earnings (HDFC Bank Q4 miss, Infosys guidance revisions ongoing — ~0.3% residual, non-crisis); Business Today/ET stale framing 'likely to open higher on moderating oil' will have to retract overnight. FPI net outflow ₹3,200cr 3 sessions + ₹17,689cr FAR G-Sec since crisis. Rupee test: OMC daily USD demand $250-300M becomes binding at Brent $95+; USD/INR Rs94.50-95 re-activates crossed-Rs95 trigger from March 27. (Sources: NSE Friday close, markets.md C2, intel.md A3/A7)

EVENING DOWNSIDE-SKEW WIDENING ON ARAGHCHI SUNDAY OFFENSIVE POSTURE [REPORTED]. Morning brief base case: -0.6 to -1.2% Monday open -> 24,060-24,210. Evening upgrade: Araghchi's offensive Sunday X posts (not silent, not deposed, not defended by Pezeshkian — directly attacking Trump) = Iran has no single negotiating voice. Downside skew widens slightly: -1.0 to -1.5% -> 23,988-24,109. Key technical: 24,050 (200-DMA) — break below intraday triggers algo selling. Sunday Globex Brent ~$98 holds -> imported-inflation tail re-engages within 18 hours of Monday open. OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL): expect -2 to -3.5% on Brent gap + Reliance Derya/Lenore compliance benchmark. Adani Ports: Mundra/Krishnapatnam Iran-Gulf exposure = -1.5 to -2.5% if Brent holds $98. Bank Nifty: direction depends on yield curve — India 10Y G-Sec gapping through 7.00% = banks -1 to -2%. Reversibility: US delegation named in Islamabad + no further IRGC kinetic by Tuesday close = Nifty recovers Friday's +0.6% by end of week.

NIFTY MONDAY BASE -0.5 TO -1.0% (24,110-24,230) — REVISED MILDER THAN EVENING'S -1.0 TO -1.5% [REPORTED]. Driver for revision: Russian-oil waiver RENEWED (not in evening framing) preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow to Indian refiners; Brent Monday cash ~$95.42 is $3-4 BELOW Globex $98 that drove evening forecasts; HDFC Bank + ICICI Bank reported Saturday April 18 (read pending). Triple headwind confirmed (not evaporating): Brent ~$95 imported-inflation tail, MEA summons political-nexus friction (40+ hrs no Iran MFA response), Iranian waiver elapsed 05:30 IST (zero marginal Iranian supply). Supportive crosscurrent: Russian-waiver renewal + potential earnings ballast. 200-DMA 24,050 likely holds intraday. OMCs -1.5 to -2.5% on Brent gap; Reliance neutral (refining-margin-beneficiary vs oil-sourcing-complexity penalty).

NIFTY FLAT MONDAY (24,364.85 +0.05%) — THE STRUCTURAL RESILIENCE SIGNAL OF THE TAPE [CONFIRMED STRUCTURAL DATA]. Sensex 78,520.30 (+0.00%); Bank Nifty 56,582.35 (+0.00%). Pre-open expectations called for -0.5 to -1.0% on Brent +5%, Europe -1.1%, US -0.24%, USD/INR ₹93.05 weakening. Nifty closed FLAT — the print IS the signal. Mechanism: cumulative FII outflow Rs 1.61 lakh crore (~$17.3B at ₹93.05) since Feb 28 (Business Today April 20) fully offset by domestic MF buying; BSE market cap at pre-war levels despite 8-week kinetic phase. ICICI Bank Q4 absorbed crisis-premium without margin compression. RBI 5.25% April 8 hold + NDF ban + NOP cap + $3.6B April intervention = regulatory layer. 200-DMA trigger (~24,050): STRUCTURALLY INSULATED — activation requires compounding event (Wed ceasefire lapse + domestic earnings miss + FII acceleration), not single shock. For investor thesis: this LOWERS left-tail probability but RAISES deployment-opportunity hurdle. Domestic absorption has 3-6 month structural persistence post-crisis-resolution via SIP inflows. Correct-sourcing set: BS + BT + Livemint (structural) + The Hindu (diplomatic); avoid Republic/Times Now/Moneycontrol-on-energy (per user feedback).

NIFTY SOFT OPEN 24,413 → INTRADAY LOW 24,241 → 24,411 BY 11:51 IST [CONFIRMED, NSE live]. Despite Trump ceasefire extension and Brent -$2.40/bbl vs Monday, Indian equities opened soft. Sensex -0.7% at 78,706. Bank Nifty flat. IT sector 'tumble' (Infosys/HCL/Wipro) on US recession-risk transmission. Tested 50-day EMA support at 24,240 zone; held. Breakdown vectors: profit-booking after Tuesday's +0.87% rally; FII continuation (Rs 48,213 crore through April 10 NSDL, Rs 1.8 lakh crore FY26 Livemint); IT weakness on US macro fear; rupee decoupling from oil relief. Key levels: support 24,240 (50-day EMA, BEING TESTED); resistance 24,576 (Tue close, rejected once); structural watch 21,500 (200-day EMA trigger).

NIFTY 24,378 (-0.8% WED CLOSE); SENSEX 78,516 (-1.0%); BANK NIFTY 57,124 (-0.4%) — CAPITAL-FLIGHT DECOUPLING DAY 3 SEALED [CONFIRMED]. Three sessions of evidence the rupee is decoupled from oil. Brent roundtripped $90.38 → $101.97 → $92.48 → $99.81 across 4 sessions; rupee monotonically WEAKER ₹92.84 → ₹93.05 → ₹93.50 → ₹93.78. ~50% crisis component (FII outflows continuing; Tuesday FPIs net seller ₹1,919 cr, DIIs ALSO net seller ₹2,221 cr — structurally bearish setup; cumulative ₹1.61 lakh crore since Feb 28); ~50% domestic component (HCL Tech tumbled ~9% on March-quarter earnings miss dragging Infosys/HCL/Wipro/Axis Bank/Apollo Healthcare). Bank Nifty -0.4% (less hit than IT-heavy indices) — ICICI Q4 NIM-protection pricing dominates IT-export weakness. Reversibility: crisis component reverses on Mojtaba endorsement + Brent <$92 sustained; domestic component (HCL Tech earnings, Fed hawkish, Q4 guidance weak) does NOT reverse on Iran resolution. Even clean ceasefire renewal Sunday only recovers ~50% of cumulative drawdown. Tifani INDOPACOM seizure (April 21) in Bay-of-Bengal is geographically meaningful but no public Indian government response — strategic-autonomy posture extends to maritime adjacency.

NIFTY 23,897.95 (-1.14%) — DAY 5 DECLINE; BROKE 24,000; ₹94.23 WEAKEST-OF-CRISIS RUPEE; FPI YTD ₹1.67T [CONFIRMED]. Nifty 50 closed 23,897.95 (-1.14%, -275 points) — Day 5 of decline, broke 24,000 psychological floor. April 9 low 23,775 now only ~0.5% below Friday close; this is the key algorithmic-selling level. Sensex 76,664.21 (-1.29%); Bank Nifty 56,089.75 (-0.4%, relatively resilient — selling concentrated in FII-heavy large-caps). USD/INR ₹94.23 (+0.5% rupee weaker); India 10Y 6.98% (+3bps); S&P 500 7,108.40 (-0.4% off Thursday record); NASDAQ 24,438.50 (-0.9%). FPI April-MTD / YTD outflows: ₹1.67 trillion (~$18.5B) — approaching April 2020 pandemic-scale capitulation territory. Thursday FII outflow alone ~₹3,255 crore, fourth session of net selling. BSE IT index lead loser Friday; oil-marketing companies drag; mid/small cap pressure sustained; pharma/healthcare relative safe havens. Brent fell ~$10/bbl two sessions but rupee STILL weakened — CONFIRMS rupee is capital-flow-driven, not oil-import-bill-driven. Primary chain: Brent >$100 sustained + sticky oil-import bill + rupee structurally weaker → FII rotation out of EM risk → Top Nifty50 losers IT + consumer; Bank Nifty relatively resilient. Secondary ~0.4%: Q4 FY26 IT weakness (Infosys, HDFC Bank, Tech Mahindra); rising US Treasury yields compound FII rotation into DM debt. Monday Nifty 23,775 break activates systematic algo selling — clean contrarian entry on weekend kinetic gap-down. Break and HOLD of 23,500 Monday = 'blood in the streets' for the 3-5 year deployment window. (Sources: Business Standard April 24 closing coverage; BusinessToday BT Closing Bell; scripts/market-data.py; RBI weekly statistical supplement)

BRANCH 3 RESOLUTION → NIFTY MONDAY OPEN SETUP REBOUND; INDIA BEST-POSITIONED OUTCOME [REPORTED]. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia open — Branch 3 (positioning) defeats Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical band). Nifty cash open setup: 23,775 floor likely HOLDS; rebound +0.5-1.5% into 24,000-24,200 area (60% probability); the Day 5 decline likely arrested. USD/INR: Brent gap-down + Branch 3 capital-flow improvement → likely tests ₹93.80-94.10 strengthening band (vs Friday ₹94.22). India 10Y: inflation-expectation easing → likely 6.80-6.85% (vs Friday 6.87% per WS data / 6.98% per Saturday script — discrepancy resolved with Reuters relay). OMCs IOC/BPCL/HPCL: diesel margin recovery as crude input cost compresses → likely +1.5-3%; sustained ₹100+ Brent regime preparation (RBI special-credit-facility for OMC dollar buying per Reuters April 26 ask) becomes lower-burden under Branch 3. Asian Monday tape: Nikkei +1.4%, KOSPI +2.2%, Taiwan TAIEX +2.7% — chip-led, supportive of Indian IT services Q1 FY27 sentiment as global semi capex remains intact. FII flow: Elara Capital Friday +$106M FPI inflow (first positive in 7 weeks) likely consolidates under Branch 3 architecture; weekly outflow decelerating $1.2B → $180M may flip to net inflow into May. The 23,775 contrarian deployment trigger is now LOW-PROBABILITY for this cycle — investor on conviction-after-mapping framework can deploy into 24,000-24,200 area Branch 3 rebound. Falsification path: surprise Hormuz kinetic intra-day reverses Brent → Nifty 23,775 retest. (Sources: Senior Market dossier Branch 3; market data; Reuters April 26 RBI special-credit-facility ask)
